İş süreçlerini ve karar verme süreçlerini desteklemek için bilgi teknolojilerini etkin bir biçimde kullanabilir olmak
Eleştirel düşünme, verileri analiz etme ve yorumlama, sorunları belirleme ve kanıta dayalı çözümler üretme becerisine sahip olmak
Bilgi fikir ve önerilerini etkin bir şekilde ifade etme, programın uygulandığı dilde (Almanca / İngilizce) yazılı ve sözlü sunum yapma yeteneğine sahip olmak
Etik değerlere, sosyal sorumluluk ve sürdürülebilirlik bilincine sahip olmak

courses.name: Introduction to Accounting
courses.purpose: This course is designed for prospective accountants as well as potential managers and others who need to understand the role of accounting in providing useful information for economic decision-making. Its main purpose is to introduce the basic function of accounting and its use in financial information.
courses.description: Accounting as an information system; users and uses of financial statements; processing economic data; income measurement; completion of the accounting cycle; accounting for merchandising activities.
learning_outcomes: Record transactions in the journal and post journal entries to the ledger.
Prepare a trial balance.
Prepare an income statement.
Prepare a balance sheet.
Record basic adjusting and closing entries.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Definition of accounting, users of accounting information, types of businesses, transactions, financial statements
Hafta 2: Balance sheet, income statement, balance sheet equation
Hafta 4: Debit-credit chart of accounts, revenue and expense accounts, example
Hafta 5: Recording transactions, account balances, trial balances
Hafta 10: Merchandise transactions, periodic inventory method
Hafta 12: Completion of the accounting cycle, year-end adjustments, depreciation, example
Hafta 14: Accrued expenses, accrued revenues, cost deferrals
resources: Financial Accounting, Jerry J. Weygandt, Donald E. Kieso, Paul D. Kimmel, Wiley

courses.name: Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılap Tarihi I
courses.purpose: Atatürk’ün eserleri incelenerek Cumhuriyetin temel nitelikleri ve elde edilen kazanımların kavratılması amaçlanır. Ayrıca Türk Devrimi’nin tamamlanma evresindeki siyasi gelişmeler ve yeni devletin kuruluş süreci ele alınır.
courses.description: Bu ders, Modern Türkiye’nin doğuş ve gelişim sürecini belirleyen tarihsel olaylar, düşünsel akımlar ve temel ilkeleri ele alır. Atatürk’ün eserleri ve ilkeleri çerçevesinde Cumhuriyetin temel nitelikleri, elde edilen kazanımlar ile Türk Devrimleri ve bu süreçte yaşanan siyasi gelişmeler incelenir.
learning_outcomes: Atatürk ilkeleri ile ilgili kaynakları tanımış olmak
Osmanlı devletinde yenilik hareketlerini kavramış olmak
Atatürk ilkelerinin tarihi temellerini kavramış olmak
Türkiye Cumhuriyeti´nin devlet yapısını iyi bir şekilde kavramış olmak
21. Yüzyılda Türklerin durumunu kavramış olmak

Hafta 4: TANZİMAT DÖNEMİ VE I. MEŞRUTİYETİN İLANI
Hafta 5: II. MEŞRUTİYET DÖNEMİ, TRABLUSGARP ve BALKAN SAVAŞLARI

courses.name: Introduction to Business
courses.purpose: The main purpose of this course is to introduce the importance of organizations in society and to explore what businesses are.
courses.description: The course covers business systems, workforce demographics, social responsibility, business ethics, forms of business organizations, entrepreneurship, small business and franchise systems, management processes, human resources management, marketing management, business finance, and business decision making.
learning_outcomes: Students will have the competence to analyze and solve problems that arise in the business management process.
Students will know which processes are involved in the functioning of business functions and will be able to make decisions about the effectiveness level of these functions.
Students will have a strong knowledge of business functions.
Students will be able to define business concepts clearly and completely and demonstrate the dynamism and vitality of commercial organizations and the people who run them with real-life examples.
At the end of this course, students will have a broad business perspective; strengths, weaknesses, successes, failures, problems, and challenges.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Business and General Business Concepts
Hafta 2: Business Environment and Types of Businesses
Hafta 6: Human Resources Management in Businesses-1
Hafta 7: Human Resources Management in Businesses-2
Hafta 15: New Concerns in the Business World
resources: Introduction to Business by K. Mirze
Last edition of HBR Journal
Capital Magazine
Suggested Readings

courses.name: Business Mathematics I
courses.purpose: This course is designed to provide students with the tools and techniques needed to understand and apply fundamental mathematical topics such as functions, limits, derivatives, and financial mathematics. It also aims to develop mathematical thinking and problem-solving skills.
courses.description: Functions, limits and continuity, derivatives, Newton's method, financial mathematics.
learning_outcomes: Explains the concepts of limits and continuity, calculates limits, and uses theorems related to limits.
Explains the concept of derivatives, calculates derivatives, uses theorems related to derivatives, and solves economics or business-related problems using derivatives.
Applies problem-solving techniques to solve complex mathematics problems related to work by developing analytical and logical reasoning skills.

Applies mathematical concepts to solve practical problems encountered in various business scenarios such as profit and loss calculations, pricing strategies, inventory management, break-even analysis, and budgeting.
Uses mathematical techniques and formulas used in financial calculations, including simple and compound interest, annuities, present value, future value, and time value of money.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Applications of equations, linear inequalities, applications of inequalities, sequences
Hafta 2: Functions, special functions, function combinations
Hafta 6: Nonlinear systems, applications of systems of equations
Hafta 12: Demand elasticity, derivative of implicit function, logarithmic differentiation, Newton's method
Hafta 13: Financial mathematics: compound interest, present value, continuous compound interest
resources: Ernest F Haeussler, Richard S. Paul, Introductory Mathematical Analysis for Business, Economics, and the Life and Social Sciences, Global Edition, Published 2021

courses.name: Introduction to Information Systems and Technology
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach the design of information systems, ethical principles, collaboration and organizational strategies, and the design of reliable decision-making systems through proper information management and the use of business intelligence.
courses.description: This course covers information systems used in businesses, collaboration and e-commerce, ethical rules in information systems design, infrastructure-appropriate system design, emerging technologies, business intelligence, information management, decision-making systems, and project management.
learning_outcomes: Performs analysis of information systems.
Designs an information system suitable for the business.
Knows the ethical and legal rules of information systems.
Operates decision processes that will increase the efficiency of the business.
Takes part in system design suitable for the business and/or organizational infrastructure.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Global businesses and information systems
Hafta 2: E-commerce and collaboration in global businesses
Hafta 3: Information systems, organization and strategy
Hafta 4: Ethical and social issues in information systems
Hafta 5: Information technology infrastructure and emerging technologies
Hafta 6: Business intelligence: databases and information management
Hafta 7: Telecommunications, internet and wireless technologies
Hafta 10: Achieving operational excellence and customer intimacy
Hafta 11: E-commerce: digital marketing and digital products
resources: Management Information Systems: Managing the Digital Firm (15th edition), Laudon & Laudon.

courses.name: Türk Dili I
courses.purpose: Türk dilinin tarihsel gelişimini ve Türkiye Türkçesinin dil özelliklerini öğretmek.
courses.description: Türk dilinin tarihsel gelişimi ve Türkiye Türkçesi dil özellikleri.

Öğrenci Türkiye Türkçesinin dil özelliklerini bilir.
Öğrenci anlatım türlerinin ayırımına varır.
Öğrenci eleştirel düşünceyi yapıcı bir şekilde ifade etme ve inisiyatif kullanma becerisi edinir.
Öğrenci sözlü ve yazılı iletişim becerisi kazanır.

courses.name: Financial Accounting
courses.purpose: This course is designed for prospective accountants as well as potential managers and others who need to understand the role of accounting in providing useful information for economic decision-making. Its main purpose is to help students understand how financial information is used in business decisions.
courses.description: The course covers financial accounting topics including cash and cash equivalents, foreign currency and check accounting, accounting for marketable securities, accounting for doubtful accounts and notes receivable, inventory valuation methods, returns and allowances, and accounting for fixed assets and special expenditures.
learning_outcomes: Record period-end adjusting entries related to cash and cash equivalents.
Record period-end adjusting entries related to marketable securities.
Record period-end adjusting entries related to receivables.
Record period-end adjusting entries related to inventory.
Record period-end adjusting entries related to fixed assets.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Cash on hand, cash shortages, petty cash
Hafta 2: Foreign currency accounting, check accounting
Hafta 3: Accounting for marketable securities; stock investments, accrued stock income, stock sale
Hafta 4: Accounting for marketable securities; bond investments, accrued bond interest, bond sale
Hafta 5: Accounting for doubtful accounts; uncollectibles, doubtful accounts
Hafta 6: Accounting for notes and notes receivable; receiving, issuing, endorsing, etc.
Hafta 9: Inventory transactions; damaged goods, weighted average, FIFO, LIFO
Hafta 10: Inventory transactions; continuation of weighted average, FIFO, LIFO
Hafta 11: Accounting for returns, allowances and discounts
Hafta 12: Accounting for fixed assets; depreciation, sale of fixed assets
Hafta 13: Accounting for fixed assets; replacement, capitalized expenditures
resources: Financial Accounting, Jerry J. Weygandt, Donald E. Kieso, Paul D. Kimmel, Wiley

courses.name: Microeconomics
courses.purpose: The main purpose of this course is to teach students the fundamental concepts and principles of microeconomics, such as supply and demand, elasticity, marginal cost, and marginal utility.
courses.description: This course covers supply and demand, elasticity, marginal cost, marginal utility, consumer behavior, and producer behavior.
learning_outcomes: Develops a solid understanding of fundamental microeconomic concepts such as supply and demand, elasticity, cost structures, utility maximization, market power, and competition.

Applies economic models and tools to analyze and understand various economic situations. This includes determining market equilibrium, calculating equilibrium price and quantity, and analyzing consumer and firm behavior.
Defines indifference curves and explains the properties of the budget line.
Defines the production function and the Law of Diminishing Marginal Returns; calculates short-run and long-run production costs and can show them graphically.
Analyzes government policies to reduce market failures such as monopoly power, externalities, and public goods.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Mikroekonominin temel kavram ve ilkelerine giriş
Hafta 5: Bireysel talep ve piyasa talebi
Hafta 13: Piyasa Yapıları: Tam rekabet, tekel, oligopolistik rekabet gibi farklı piyasa biçimlerinin analizi
Hafta 14: Piyasa Yapıları: Tam rekabet, tekel, oligopolistik rekabet gibi farklı piyasa biçimlerinin analizi
Hafta 15: Piyasa başarısızlıkları: serbest piyasanın verimli bir şekilde işlemediği durumları incelemek.

Robert S. Pindyck, Daniel L. Rubinfeld (2009), Mikroökonomie,7. Aufl., Pearson

courses.name: Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılap Tarihi II
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, Atatürk’ün eserleri doğrultusunda Cumhuriyetin temel niteliklerini ve elde edilen kazanımları öğrencilere aktararak, Atatürk ilkelerinin tarihsel ve ideolojik değerini kavratmaktır. Ayrıca, Türk Devrimi’nin tamamlanma sürecinde yaşanan siyasi gelişmeler ile Türkiye Cumhuriyeti’nin kuruluş süreci incelenerek, öğrencilerin modern Türkiye’nin oluşum dinamiklerini tarihsel bir perspektifle değerlendirmeleri hedeflenmektedir.
courses.description: Modern Türkiye’nin doğuş ve gelişim sürecinde etkili olan tarihsel olaylar, düşünsel akımlar ve kurucu ilkeler ele alınır. Cumhuriyet’in ilanı, Atatürk dönemi reformları, iç ve dış politik gelişmeler ile Türkiye’nin çağdaşlaşma süreci kapsamlı şekilde incelenir.
learning_outcomes: Türk inkılâbının tarihî köklerine inerek, Atatürk inkılâplarına bilinçli bir şekilde sahip çıkar.
Türk tarihinden ve Türk millî mücadelesinden aldığı güçle devletine ve milletine sahip çıkar.
Cumhuriyete, lâikliğe ve Türk milletine yönelik iç ve dış tehditleri doğru bir şekilde tanıyarak, bu tehditler karşısında bilinçli ve duyarlı bir tutum geliştirir.
Ülkesini, vatanını ve milletini tanıyarak, onlara uygun politikalar üreten bir şuura sahip olur.
TBMM’nin kurulmasında yaşanan güçlükleri bilir.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: DEVRİMLERE KARŞI OLUŞAN TEPKİLER VE ÇOK PARTİLİ SİSTEME GEÇİŞ DENEMELERİ
Hafta 4: KÜLTÜR VE SOSYAL YAŞAM ALANINDAKİ DEVRİMLER
Hafta 6: ATATÜRK DÖNEMİ TÜRK DIŞ POLİTİKASI I
Hafta 7: ATATÜRK DÖNEMİ TÜRK DIŞ POLİTİKASI II

Hafta 12: ATATÜRK SONRASI İÇ VE DIŞ POLİTİK GELİŞMELER

courses.name: Macroeconomics
courses.purpose: This course is designed to teach macroeconomic relationships such as the labor market, goods market, money market, and foreign exchange market, as well as macroeconomic indicators and measurements, the determinants of economic growth, and the causes and effects of business cycle fluctuations.
courses.description: This course covers the goods market, money and financial markets, the IS-LM model, the labor market, the AS-AD model, the Phillips curve, money supply growth, inflation, and output.
learning_outcomes: To learn the concept of Gross Domestic Product (GDP), its components, and calculation methods.
To understand the concept of unemployment, its functioning, and labor market dynamics.
To explain the relationship between the money system and inflation.
To understand the effects of fiscal and monetary policies on the economy.
To gain critical thinking and problem-solving skills in macroeconomic issues.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Definition and scope of macroeconomics, overview of national economies
Hafta 2: Goods market, components of GDP: consumption, investment, government spending, exports and imports
Hafta 4: Money markets: money supply and demand, interest rates and central bank policies
Hafta 5: Money demand: the relationship between money supply and demand
Hafta 7: Goods market and IS equation, money market and LM equation
Hafta 10: An overview of the labor market
Hafta 12: Aggregate Demand, Aggregate Supply Model
Hafta 15: Phillips Curve, inflation, expected inflation, and unemployment
resources: Makroökonomie, 6., aktualisierte Auflage, Olivier Blanchard, Gerhard Illing, Pearson.

courses.name: Business Mathematics II
courses.purpose: This course is designed to provide students with the tools and techniques needed to understand and apply basic mathematical topics such as extrema, integrals, and matrix algebra. It also aims to develop mathematical thinking, problem-solving skills, and logical reasoning.
courses.description: Relative extrema, absolute extrema on a closed interval, concavity, differentials, integral, matrix algebra.
learning_outcomes: Applies mathematical techniques to draw and interpret curves.
Defines the differential, interprets it geometrically, and uses it to estimate variables in economic applications.
Recognizes mathematical methods used in computer science.
Can calculate definite and indefinite integrals of a function and solve economic or business problems using integrals.
Can examine the solution of a system of linear equations using the Gaussian elimination method.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Finding relative maximum and minimum values, absolute extrema on a closed interval
Hafta 6: Differentials, indefinite integral, integral with initial conditions, integration techniques
Hafta 7: Curves, area between consumer and producer surplus
Hafta 11: Matrix addition and scalar multiplication, matrix multiplication
Hafta 12: Solving systems by matrix reduction
resources: Ernest F Haeussler, Richard S. Paul, Introductory Mathematical Analysis for Business, Economics, and the Life and Social Sciences, Global Edition, Published 2021

courses.name: Türk Dili II
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, sözlü ve yazılı anlatım türlerini öğretmek; öğrencilerin eleştirel düşüncelerini yapıcı bir biçimde ifade edebilme ve inisiyatif kullanabilme becerilerini geliştirmek; sözlü ve yazılı iletişim yetkinliği kazandırmaktır.
courses.description: Bu derste, başta hikâye, roman, şiir, tiyatro olmak üzere çeşitli yazılı edebi türler ile anı, biyografi, deneme, makale, gezi yazısı ve röportaj gibi gazetecilik ve düşünce yazıları incelenir.
learning_outcomes: Öğrenci yazılı anlatım (edebi) türlerini bilir.
Öğrenci sözlü anlatım türlerini bilir.
Öğrenci bilgiyi kullanabileceği çalışmalar yapabilir.
Öğrenci eleştirel düşünceyi yapıcı bir şekilde ifade etme ve inisiyatif kullanma becerisi edinir.
Öğrenci sözlü ve yazılı iletişim becerisi kazanır.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Literary genres@ Story and novel.
Hafta 4: Literary genres@ Memory and biography.
Hafta 5: Literary genres@ Essay, review and newspaper article.

courses.name: Introduction to Algorithms and Data Structures
courses.purpose: This course provides a comprehensive introduction to programming concepts using the C# language, with an emphasis on procedural programming. It covers the fundamentals of computer programming and the syntax of C#, while not delving into object-oriented programming concepts.
courses.description: This course begins with a general overview of programming and computer science, then introduces the C# language and the .NET framework to build a basic understanding of programming. The focus then shifts to C# syntax and understanding variables, operators, and expressions.
learning_outcomes: Understand fundamental programming concepts
Gain proficiency in C# syntax
Write basic console programs
Manage control flow in a program
Handle errors and exceptions

Hafta 4: Değişkenler, işleçler ve ifadeler - Bölüm 1
Hafta 5: Değişkenler, işleçler ve ifadeler - Bölüm 2

courses.name: Fundamentals of Finance
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students the fundamentals of the finance discipline, including financial statements, analysis, and other basic concepts and techniques, so that corporate finance can be effectively understood.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamentals of financial management, including financial markets, institutions, instruments, financial statements and analysis, interest rates, TVM, risk and return, bond and stock valuation, and capital budgeting techniques.
learning_outcomes: Understand the financial environment, financial markets, and instruments
Understand and use financial statements for managerial purposes such as financial analysis and performance evaluation
Understand and use interest rates for time value calculations and bond/stock valuation
Learn about risk and return and their effects on portfolio investments and corporate decisions
Understand and use capital budgeting techniques such as NPV, IRR, MIRR, and PI

Hafta 2: Financial statements, their interactions, and the use of accounting information
Hafta 3: Financial analysis and cash flow statement

Hafta 5: Interest rates: determination, base rates, risk premiums, and yield curves
Hafta 9: Risk and return, standard deviation, coefficient of variation, and portfolios
Hafta 10: CAPM, beta, and determination of required rate of return
Hafta 11: Bonds, bond market, and bond valuation
Hafta 12: Stocks and valuation, DCF methodology
Hafta 13: Stock valuation: relative and contingent valuation
Hafta 14: Capital budgeting techniques, discounted payback period, NPV, IRR, MIRR, PI

courses.name: Computer Hardware and System Software
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach the operating principles of computers, the structure and functions of hardware, and the software architectures of operating systems.
courses.description: This course covers computer hardware, processing data in machine language, number systems, computer architecture, processor and memory management, computer networks, and operating system topics.
learning_outcomes: Understands the operating principles of computers.
Recognizes processor architectures.
Knows the machine-language representation of data.
Recognizes modern computer systems.
Knows operating system architectures.

Hafta 13: Computer network protocols and layers

courses.name: Business Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with knowledge about the classical functions of management, emerging trends in management, and the basic principles and theories of management.
courses.description: This basic management course examines the different functions of management, namely planning, organizing, leading, and controlling. The course also covers organizational structure, technology, innovation, ethics, corporate responsibility, organizational culture, and the competitive environment.
learning_outcomes: Understand different management functions and their applications in organizations.
Critically examine the managerial decision-making process, its reasons, and the context in which it takes place in order to make informed decisions and sharpen one's own judgment.
Analyze various management-related topics such as social responsibility, ethics, global management, leadership, organizational change, entrepreneurship, and human resource management.

Hafta 4: Corporate Culture and Global Environment
Hafta 10: Behavioral Dynamics and Leadership in Organizations
Hafta 11: Employee Motivation, Teams and Communication

courses.name: Object Oriented Programming
courses.purpose: This course is designed to explore the intricacies of object-oriented programming in depth, with a special focus on advanced programming techniques in C#. It aims to build a strong foundation in the core principles of object-oriented programming and their practical use.
courses.description: The course introduces the fundamental concepts of object-oriented programming, beginning with a transition from functional to object-oriented paradigms. It covers the creation of value types using enums and structs, the distinction between value and reference types, arrays and parameter arrays, inheritance, interfaces, abstract classes, garbage collection and resource management, properties, bitwise operations and indexers, generics, collections, operator overloading, and practical exercises involving query expressions.

courses.name: Business Statistics I
courses.purpose: Modern businesses and contemporary managers use quantitative methods to reduce uncertainty in the environment and support better decision-making. This course aims to teach students how to classify, summarize, analyze, and present collected data in order to obtain information that increases the probability of making correct decisions. It also helps students understand the theoretical properties of inferential statistical methods and develop practical skills for analyzing business data and drawing conclusions about populations from sample data.
courses.description: This course covers data types and data collection, methods for describing data sets, graphical methods, measures of central tendency and variability, methods for detecting outliers and other descriptive techniques, probability, distributions for discrete random variables including binomial, Poisson, and hypergeometric distributions, probability distributions for continuous random variables including normal distribution, other continuous distributions such as uniform and exponential, sampling distribution and the central limit theorem. It also covers inference based on a single sample, confidence interval estimation, hypothesis testing, inference based on two samples, confidence intervals and hypothesis tests, design of experiments and analysis of variance, categorical data analysis, simple linear regression, multiple regression, and model building.
learning_outcomes: Learn a wide variety of data analysis techniques
Develop statistical thinking to evaluate the reliability and value of inferences made from data
Learn how to evaluate data and make informed business decisions
Learn how to build models for decision-making
Learn how to use statistics effectively in a business environment

Hafta 6: Discrete random variables - Poisson distribution and hypergeometric distribution

Hafta 11: Inferences based on two samples: confidence intervals and hypothesis tests
Hafta 15: Multiple regression and model building

courses.name: Special Topics in MIS I
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of software engineering practices, with a particular focus on software implementation and testing in a financial context. It also introduces key concepts in computer networking, client-server application development, API usage, software development lifecycle models, and agile principles.
courses.description: The course covers software engineering applications in detail, starting with an introduction to computer networks, OSI reference model, network topologies, and communication protocols such as UDP and TCP. It then moves on to software development in a financial context, including APIs, socket programming, client-server architecture, and special protocols such as FIX and OUCH. Students also learn memory management, garbage collection, object pooling, efficient client-server application development, SDLC, and testing methods such as unit, integration, smoke, load, and SLA testing. Agile principles including Scrum, Kanban, and TDD, as well as clean code, defensive programming, refactoring, and version control systems, are also covered.
learning_outcomes: Students will gain a solid understanding of computer networking concepts, including the OSI model, network topologies, and communication protocols such as UDP, TCP, and the TCP/IP stack.

Students will learn to design and implement efficient client-server applications using industry-standard architectures and protocols, and gain practical experience with APIs and financial communication protocols such as FIX and OUCH.
Students will develop the ability to optimize software applications by applying advanced memory management techniques, efficient object creation, and effective use of collections and libraries.

Students will learn about the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and gain proficiency in testing methods such as unit testing, integration testing, smoke testing, and load testing to build robust and reliable software solutions.

Students will understand and apply agile principles such as Scrum, Kanban, and TDD, while adhering to practices such as clean code, defensive programming, refactoring techniques, and version control to develop sustainable and high-quality software.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Introduction to Computer Networks - Basic concepts, OSI reference model (from Physical Layer to Application Layer), and network topologies.
Hafta 3: Network Communication Protocols - Comparison of UDP and TCP, TCP/IP stack, introduction to network tools (Pcap, Wireshark).
Hafta 4: Software Development in the Financial Domain - APIs, socket programming, and an overview of client-server architecture.
Hafta 5: Financial Communication Protocols - In-depth look at the use of FIX API and OUCH API in financial systems.
Hafta 6: Client-Server Application Development - Designing and implementing client-server architectures with practical examples.
Hafta 7: Memory Management and Optimization - Java memory management, garbage collection, string usage, and efficient object handling.
Hafta 9: Application Efficiency Techniques - Code optimization with appropriate collections, object pooling, and logging libraries.
Hafta 10: Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) and Introduction to Testing - Overview of SDLC, test types (unit, integration, smoke, load, and SLA tests).
Hafta 11: Unit Test and Integration Test - Best practices and tools for testing software components and integrations.
Hafta 12: Agile Principles and Methodologies - Introduction to agility, Scrum, Kanban, XP, and comparison with the Waterfall model.
Hafta 13: Test-Driven Development (TDD) and Agile Practices - Practical applications of TDD and other agile principles in software development.
Hafta 14: Clean Code and Version Control Systems - Clean code principles, defensive programming, refactoring techniques, and version control tools (e.g., Git).
Hafta 15: Final Case Study and Course Wrap-Up - Application of all learned concepts to a real case study in a financial domain and summary of course objectives.
